title: viola × modica
intro: Viola × modica is a hybrid plant, resulting from the cross between Viola tricolor and Viola betulae, a species of pansy in the family Violaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Viola × modica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
